title: Quiz: Energy Materials
description: Test your understanding of computational energy materials design

questions.question: How is the voltage of a battery electrode calculated from DFT?

options.text: From the band gap of the electrode
From the Gibbs free energy difference of lithiation
From the work function of the material

questions.explanation: Battery voltage V = -ΔG/(nF), where ΔG is the Gibbs free energy change for lithiation/delithiation. At T=0, this is approximated by the DFT total energy difference between lithiated and delithiated states minus the Li metal reference.

questions.question: What is the optimal band gap for a single-junction solar cell?
questions.explanation: The Shockley-Queisser limit shows maximum efficiency (~33%) at E_g ≈ 1.34 eV for AM1.5 solar spectrum. Materials with gaps of 1.1-1.5 eV (like Si at 1.1 eV, GaAs at 1.4 eV) are optimal.

questions.question: In thermoelectrics, which quantity should be maximized for high ZT?
questions.explanation: ZT = S²σT/κ. To maximize ZT, increase the power factor S²σ (Seebeck coefficient squared times conductivity) while minimizing thermal conductivity κ, especially the lattice contribution κ_L.

questions.question: For practical hydrogen storage in metal hydrides, what is the optimal formation enthalpy?
questions.explanation: Optimal ΔH is -30 to -40 kJ/mol H₂. Too negative (like MgH₂ at -75) requires high temperature to release H₂. Too close to zero gives poor capacity at ambient conditions.

questions.question: NEB (Nudged Elastic Band) calculations are used to find ion migration barriers in solid electrolytes.
questions.explanation: True. NEB is the standard method for finding minimum energy paths and transition states for ion migration. It provides the activation energy E_a that determines diffusivity through D = D₀ exp(-E_a/kT).

questions.question: Which are valid strategies to reduce lattice thermal conductivity in thermoelectrics? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Alloying, nanostructuring, and rattling atoms all scatter phonons and reduce κ_L. Increasing band gap affects electronic properties but doesn't directly reduce lattice thermal conductivity.
